signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.48 — 289bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry premium sustains the bid, while Japan Q1 capex growth stalled at near zero, raising GDP revision risk and BoJ rate hike uncertainty. JPY COT LF net short −86,249 (extreme spec short) vs BoJ hike bias for the next meeting. This creates a significant short-squeeze risk if BoJ signals a hawkish shift. Middle East tensions introduce intervention risk on a sustained break above 160.00. Options market 25d RR at −1.04 (moderate put skew) suggests options traders are hedging for JPY strength, diverging from spot and futures positioning. Watch for BoJ's next meeting on 16 Jun for clarity on policy and potential intervention triggers.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Hawkish Shift + Volatility Premium
signals.text: ECB’s Schnabel strengthened the case for a June hike, citing inflation expectations risk. This hawkish commentary supports EUR despite the 169bp OIS carry disadvantage (SOFR 3.62% vs €STR 1.93%) favoring USD. EUR/USD IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.2% (VRP +2.9%) indicates elevated options market hedging demand above realized volatility, suggesting caution. EUR COT LF net short −26,311 (week ending 2026-05-26) is a moderate short position. Watch for the ECB meeting on 11 Jun for confirmation of a hawkish shift.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −13,767 (specs fading a hawkish CB) vs RBNZ hike bias for the next meeting. If RBNZ delivers or signals a hike, short-squeeze risk is elevated. Retail sentiment shows an extreme 81% short position, reinforcing the contrarian signal against the institutional short. NZD/USD is at 0.5957. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 20% hike priced) is the catalyst for a potential short-squeeze targeting 0.6000+.

signals.title: DXY — Correlation Break + CESI Strength
signals.text: DXY vs SPX correlation at +0.565 vs 252d norm=−0.261 (z=+1.67σ) indicates a break in the normal negative relationship, suggesting USD funding stress or a risk-off USD bid despite normal volatility (VIX 15.8). USD CESI net=+20 (strong positive) confirms sustained data outperformance, supporting underlying USD strength. DXY at 99.05. Watch for further equity weakness to confirm the risk-off USD bid.
